design_philosophy.core_loop: Side-scrolling action platforming with driving segments, progressing through film-based levels as Kyle Reese
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat against Terminator machines while navigating hazardous environments
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Recreate Kyle Reese's journey across 1984 Los Angeles and future war settings from the film
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid platformer-driving game structure tied directly to film narrative; uncompromising checkpoint placement suggests difficulty-first design philosophy

difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoints placed far apart, creating significant punishment for failure

progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression tied to film narrative (2029 future war → 1984 Los Angeles police station → nightclub)
replayability_hooks: Multiple level types (platforming and driving) break up pacing
Film-based level design provides narrative motivation for replay

world_building_techniques: Level design reflects specific film locations (police station, nightclub, future war zones)
Player character Kyle Reese grounds narrative perspective
Temporal split between 1984 and 2029 settings mirrors film structure

danger_signals: Future war setting emphasizes existential threat
environmental_storytelling: 2029 future war levels visualize post-apocalyptic timeline
1984 Los Angeles locations (police station, nightclub) ground player in specific moments from film
Driving levels contrast with platforming sections, reinforcing varied gameplay contexts

sources_used: Wikipedia - List of Terminator video games (Nintendo versions section)
